evidence_fetchers_catalog.description: Catalog of all available evidence fetcher scripts

aws.name: Amazon Web Services
aws.access_method: AWS CLI via AWS SSO/read-only IAM role
aws.access_method_description: The evidence fetcher scripts use AWS CLI which must be installed and configured with a read-only AWS SSO/read-only IAM role.

auto_scaling_high_availability.name: Auto Scaling High Availability
auto_scaling_high_availability.description: Evidence for Auto Scaling group high availability configurations
auto_scaling_high_availability.instructions: Script: auto_scaling_high_availability.sh. Commands executed: aws autoscaling describe-auto-scaling-groups, aws autoscaling describe-launch-configurations
solution_capabilities: Backup Recovery: Amazon Simple Storage Service (S3)
User-Level Data Backup Configuration: Amazon RDS (Postgres)

validators.name: Minimum Assessment Scope AWS
validators.statement: Validates that all regions are within scope of the authorization and that no data is in a different region and therefore not being audited

aws_component_ssl_enforcement.name: AWS Component SSL Enforcement
aws_component_ssl_enforcement.description: Evidence for SSL/TLS enforcement across AWS components including S3 buckets and RDS instances
aws_component_ssl_enforcement.instructions: Script: aws_component_ssl_enforcement_status.sh. Commands executed: aws s3api list-buckets, aws s3api get-bucket-policy, aws rds describe-db-instances, aws rds describe-db-parameters
solution_capabilities: Denial Of Service Protection: Amazon Web Application Firewall

validators.name: RDS SSL Enforcement Status Validator
validators.statement: Validator to ensure RDS databases are enforcing SSL

validators.name: S3 SSL Enforcement Status Validator
validators.statement: Validator to ensure S3 bucket are enforcing SSL

validators.name: Minimum Assessment Scope AWS
validators.statement: Validates that all regions are within scope of the authorization and that no data is in a different region and therefore not being audited

validators.name: SSL/TLS Enforcement
validators.statement: This validator verifies that SSL/TLS (encryption in transit) is enforced for the AWS S3 buckets and RDS database instances included in the evidence JSON. It evaluates the summary counters to confirm that the evidence covers at least one S3 bucket and at least one RDS instance, then checks that enforcement is complete by requiring the number of S3 buckets with SSL/TLS enforced to equal the total number of S3 buckets evaluated (s3_ssl_enforced = s3_total) and the number of RDS instances with SSL/TLS enforced to equal the total number of RDS instances evaluated (rds_ssl_enforced = rds_total). The validator passes only when these totals match, indicating 100% SSL/TLS enforcement for all evaluated resources; it fails if any mismatch suggests at least one evaluated resource may permit non‑TLS connections and requires remediation or further review.

aws_config_conformance_packs.name: AWS Config Conformance Packs
aws_config_conformance_packs.description: Evidence for AWS Config conformance pack compliance and rule evaluations
aws_config_conformance_packs.instructions: Script: aws_config_conformance_packs.sh. Commands executed: aws configservice describe-conformance-packs, aws configservice describe-conformance-pack-status, aws configservice get-conformance-pack-compliance-summary, aws configservice get-conformance-pack-compliance-details

validators.name: Minimum Assessment Scope AWS
validators.statement: Validates that all regions are within scope of the authorization and that no data is in a different region and therefore not being audited

aws_config_monitoring.instructions: Script: aws_config_monitoring.sh. Commands executed: aws configservice describe-configuration-recorders, aws configservice describe-configuration-recorder-status, aws configservice describe-delivery-channels

validators.name: AWS Config Monitoring
validators.statement: Validates that recording is enabled and status is healthy

cloudtrail_configuration.name: CloudTrail Configuration
cloudtrail_configuration.description: Evidence for CloudTrail configuration showing audit event logging setup in AWS account/region
cloudtrail_configuration.instructions: Script: cloudtrail_configuration.sh. Commands executed: aws cloudtrail list-trails, aws cloudtrail get-trail, aws cloudtrail get-trail-status

validators.name: Minimum Assessment Scope AWS
validators.statement: Validates that all regions are within scope of the authorization and that no data is in a different region and therefore not being audited

validators.name: CloudTrail Actively Logging
validators.statement: Validates at least one CloudTrail trail exists and is actively logging. Checks the IsLogging field is true, ensuring audit data is being captured.

validators.name: CloudTrail Multi-Region Coverage
validators.statement: Validates trails have multi-region logging enabled (IsMultiRegionTrail: true). Required for API activity auditing across all AWS regions.

validators.name: CloudTrail Global Service Events Enabled
validators.statement: Validates trails include global service events (IncludeGlobalServiceEvents: true). Required for logging IAM, STS, and CloudFront API calls.

validators.name: CloudTrail Log File Validation Enabled
validators.statement: Validates log file validation is enabled (cryptographic signing). Ensures audit logs cannot be tampered with undetected.

validators.name: CloudTrail Resources in GovCloud
validators.statement: Validates all trail resources are in FedRAMP-authorized GovCloud regions. Checks TrailARN contains aws-us-gov prefix for compliance boundary enforcement.

validators.name: CloudTrail CloudWatch Logs Integration
validators.statement: Validates CloudTrail deliver logs to CloudWatch (CloudWatchLogsLogGroupArn configured). Enables real-time monitoring, alerting, and SIEM integration.

validators.name: CloudTrail Logs Encrypted with KMS
validators.statement: Validates trails use KMS encryption for log files (KmsKeyId configured). Ensures audit data is encrypted at rest in S3.

validators.name: RDS Cross-Region Backup Replication to us-gov-east-1
validators.statement: Confirms each RDS DB instance has a paired cross-region backup ARN in us-gov-east-1. If a DB instance lacks one, match count drops.

validators.name: RDS Multi-AZ Enabled
validators.statement: Validates RDS subnet groups span multiple availability zones for failover capability.

validators.name: RDS Subnet Group Multi-AZ
validators.statement: Validates RDS subnet groups span multiple availability zones for failover capability.

validators.name: RDS Pre/Dev Backup Retention (7 days)

validators.name: RDS Production Backup Retention (30 days)
validators.statement: Validates RDS production databases have backup retention period of at least 30 days.

validators.name: IAM Policies No Wildcard Resources

validators.name: IAM External IDs Configured

validators.name: KMS Key Rotation (summary)

validators.name: KMS Rotation Config Rule
validators.statement: Checks if the AWS Config rule for key rotation is passing (from cmk-backing-key-rotation-enabled-conformance-pack https://docs.aws.amazon.com/config/latest/developerguide/cmk-backing-key-rotation-enabled.html)

validators.statement: Validates that all regions are within scope of the authorization and that no data is in a different region and therefore not being audited

validators.name: All S3 Buckets Encrypted
validators.statement: Validates that all (100% of) S3 buckets are encrypted at rest

security_groups.name: Security Groups
security_groups.description: Evidence for security group configurations and rules
security_groups.instructions: Script: security_groups.sh. Commands executed: aws ec2 describe-security-groups
solution_capabilities: Policy Enforcement Points: Amazon Security Groups

validators.name: Outbound Port Allowlist
validators.statement: Outbound to 0.0.0.0/0 only on approved ports HTTP (80), HTTPS (443), Email (25, 465)

validators.name: Outbound SG Allowlist
validators.statement: Only approved SGs can have ANY outbound to 0.0.0.0/0 (sg-083f sg-09c5 and sg-0377)

validators.name: SG Inbound no 0.0.0.0/0 other than HTTPS
validators.statement: Validates that no security groups have inbound rules open to 0.0.0.0/0 on ports other than 443 (HTTPS)

validators.name: Inbound SG Allowlist for Internet-Exposed
validators.statement: Validates that only approved security groups (ALB/NLB sg-0f64, sg-0a1c and sg-05d1) have inbound rules open to 0.0.0.0/0. Any other security group with internet exposure fails.

waf_all_rules.name: WAF All Rules
waf_all_rules.description: Evidence for all WAF rules and configurations
waf_all_rules.instructions: Script: waf_all_rules.sh. Commands executed: aws wafv2 list-web-acls, aws wafv2 get-web-acl, aws wafv2 list-rule-groups
solution_capabilities: Denial Of Service Protection: Amazon Web Application Firewall

validators.name: Minimum Assessment Scope AWS
validators.statement: Validates that all regions are within scope of the authorization and that no data is in a different region and therefore not being audited

validators.name: WAF DoS Rules
validators.statement: This validator verifies that the evidence includes at least one AWS WAF WebACL entry with a Rules array present, indicating that WAF rule configuration data is being collected and available for review as part of Denial‑of‑Service protection monitoring. It does this by matching occurrences of the "Rules": [ pattern in the evidence and passing when at least one match is found, confirming the artifact contains WAF rules context

validators.name: WAF CloudWatch Metrics Enabled
validators.statement: Validates AWS WAF has CloudWatch metrics enabled for visibility into attack patterns and DoS attempts.

validators.name: WAF IP Reputation List Enabled
validators.statement: Ensures AWS WAF includes the AWS Managed IP Reputation List and/or the AWSManagedRulesAnonymousIpList to block known malicious IPs, botnets, VPNs, proxies, and tor exit nodes commonly used to flood in DoS attacks.

validators.name: WAF Known Bad Inputs Protection Enabled
validators.statement: Validates WAF includes Known Bad Inputs rule set to block request patterns associated with exploitation.

validators.name: AWS WAF Common Rule Set Enabled
validators.statement: Validates AWS WAF includes AWS Common Rule Set for protection against common web exploits including size restrictions and request flooding.

waf_dos_rules.name: WAF DoS Rules
waf_dos_rules.description: Evidence for WAF DoS protection rules and configurations
waf_dos_rules.instructions: Script: waf_DoS_rules.sh. Commands executed: aws wafv2 list-web-acls, aws wafv2 get-web-acl
solution_capabilities: Denial Of Service Protection: Amazon Web Application Firewall

validators.name: WAF DoS Rules
validators.statement: This validator verifies that the evidence includes at least one AWS WAF WebACL entry with a Rules array present, indicating that WAF rule configuration data is being collected and available for review as part of Denial‑of‑Service protection monitoring. It does this by matching occurrences of the "Rules": [ pattern in the evidence and passing when at least one match is found, confirming the artifact contains WAF rules context

sentinelone.name: SentinelOne
sentinelone.description: SentinelOne-specific evidence collection scripts and fetchers for users, agents, XDR assets, cloud detection rules, and activities.
sentinelone.access_method_description: The evidence fetcher scripts use the SentinelOne REST API which requires SENTINELONE_API_URL and SENTINELONE_API_TOKEN environment variables. Authentication uses Api Token authorization header.

sentinelone_user_config.name: SentinelOne User Config
sentinelone_user_config.description: Retrieve SentinelOne user records and produce summary analysis (admin counts, 2FA, last login).
sentinelone_user_config.instructions: Script: userfetcher.py. Calls the SentinelOne API endpoint /web/api/v2.1/users using ApiToken authorization, pages with cursor, and writes JSON and CSV summary.

validation_rules.logic: IF record_count >= 0 THEN PASS (ensures script returned numeric count)

validators.name: SentinelOne User Fetcher Validator
validators.statement: Validates that all users have 2FA enabled

sentinelone_xdr_assets.name: SentinelOne XDR Assets
sentinelone_xdr_assets.description: Retrieve XDR asset inventory (containers, images, cloud resources) and summary counts.
sentinelone_xdr_assets.instructions: Script: xdrassetfetcher.py. Calls the SentinelOne API endpoint /web/api/v2.1/xdr/assets, supports cursor pagination, summarizes infectionStatus and assetStatus, writes JSON and CSV summary.

validators.name: SentinelOne XDR Asset Fetcher Validator
validators.statement: Validates that XDR assets are healthy and active

sentinelone_agents.name: SentinelOne Agents
sentinelone_agents.description: Retrieve agents information, containerized workload counts, cloud provider metadata, and optional agent counts endpoint.
sentinelone_agents.instructions: Script: agentfetcher.py. Calls /web/api/v2.1/agents (and /web/api/v2.1/agents/count), pages with cursor, collects cluster names and container/pod counts, writes JSON and CSV summary.

validation_rules.logic: IF reported_agent_count is null or reported_agent_count >= 0 THEN PASS (optional count may be missing)

validators.name: SentinelOne Agent Fetcher Validator
validators.statement: Validates that majority of agents have completed a full successful scan

sentinelone_cloud_detection_rules.name: SentinelOne Cloud Detection Rules
sentinelone_cloud_detection_rules.description: Retrieve custom cloud detection rules, last alert times and update timestamps.
sentinelone_cloud_detection_rules.instructions: Script: clouddetectionrules.py. Calls /web/api/v2.1/cloud-detection/rules with cursor pagination, extracts lastAlertTime and updatedAt fields, writes JSON and CSV summary.

validators.name: SentinelOne Custom Cloud Detection Rules
validators.statement: Validates existence of essential custom cloud detection rules (eg: Root login, stop logging, RDS DB modification, S3 encryption deletion)
validators.regex: "s1ql":\s*"dataSource\.name = 'CloudTrail' and (?:activity_source = 'signin\.amazonaws\.com' and activity_name = 'ConsoleLogin' and actor\.user\.type = 'Root' and unmapped\.responseElements\.ConsoleLogin = '(?:Failure|Success)'|activity_source = 'cloudtrail\.amazonaws\.com' and activity_name = 'StopLogging'|activity_source = 'iam\.amazonaws\.com' and activity_name = 'CreateAccessKey'|activity_source = 'rds\.amazonaws\.com' and activity_name = 'ModifyDBInstance'|activity_source = 's3\.amazonaws\.com' and activity_name = 'DeleteBucketEncryption'|activity_source = 'iam\.amazonaws\.com' and activity_name in \('CreateLoginProfile', 'UpdateLoginProfile'\) and unmapped\.requestParameters\.passwordResetRequired = false)"

validators.name: SentinelOne Cloud Logging Sources
validators.statement: Validates ongoing ingestion of logging from multiple sources (SentinelOne agents (K8s) | AWS VPC Flow | AWS Load Balancer | S3 Access | CloudTrail Audit logs)

sentinelone_activities.name: SentinelOne Activities
sentinelone_activities.description: Retrieve SentinelOne activity events for a set of activity types (FedRAMP list) and summarize activity types and descriptions.
sentinelone_activities.instructions: Script: activityfetcher.py. Calls /web/api/v2.1/activities with activityTypes filter (cursor pagination), aggregates activityType counts and primary descriptions, writes JSON and CSV summary.

validation_rules.logic: IF activity_type object exists THEN PASS

validators.name: SentinelOne Activity Fetcher Validator
validators.statement: Validates that excessive activity is not occurring outside of normal company activity

k8s.name: Kubernetes
k8s.description: Kubernetes and EKS-specific evidence collection scripts
k8s.access_method_description: The evidence fetcher scripts use kubectl which must be installed and configured for your Kubernetes cluster. For EKS clusters, use 'aws eks update-kubeconfig' to configure kubectl.

eks_microservice_segmentation.name: EKS Microservice Segmentation
eks_microservice_segmentation.description: Evidence for EKS microservice segmentation including network policies, security groups, and resource limits
eks_microservice_segmentation.instructions: Script: eks_microservice_segmentation.sh. Commands executed: kubectl get networkpolicies, kubectl describe daemonset aws-node, aws ec2 describe-instances

eks_pod_inventory.name: EKS Pod Inventory
eks_pod_inventory.description: Evidence for EKS pod inventory and security analysis
eks_pod_inventory.instructions: Script: eks_pod_inventory.sh. Commands executed: kubectl get pods, kubectl describe pods

kubectl_security.name: Kubectl Security
kubectl_security.description: Evidence for Kubernetes security configuration validation
kubectl_security.instructions: Script: kubectl_security.sh. Commands executed: kubectl get clusterroles, kubectl get clusterrolebindings, kubectl get networkpolicies

knowbe4.name: KnowBe4
knowbe4.description: KnowBe4 security awareness training evidence collection scripts
knowbe4.access_method_description: The evidence fetcher scripts use the KnowBe4 REST API which requires KNOWBE4_API_KEY environment variable. Note: KnowBe4 API access is only available for Platinum and Diamond customers. Authentication uses Bearer token authorization.

high_risk_training.name: Role Specific Training for High Risk Users
high_risk_training.description: Evidence for role-specific training compliance and completion status
high_risk_training.instructions: Script: high_risk_training.sh. Commands executed: KnowBe4 API calls for role-specific training data

validators.name: High Risk User Training Validator
validators.statement: Confirms the completion of role-specific security training for high risk users

security_awareness_training.name: Security Awareness Training
security_awareness_training.description: Evidence for security awareness training compliance and user completion status
security_awareness_training.instructions: Script: security_awareness_training.sh. Commands executed: KnowBe4 API calls for user training data and enrollments

validators.name: Security Awareness Training Validator
validators.statement: Validates completion rate of Security Awareness Training

developer_specific_training.name: Developer Specific Training
developer_specific_training.description: Evidence for developer specific security awareness training compliance and user completion status
developer_specific_training.instructions: Script: developer_specific_training.sh. Commands executed: KnowBe4 API calls for user training data and enrollments

validators.name: Developer Specific Training Validator
validators.statement: Validates completion rate of Developer Specific Training as reported by KnowBe4 system.

module_based_summary.name: Module Based Summary
module_based_summary.description: Evidence for security awareness training summarized at the module level
module_based_summary.instructions: Script: module_based_summary.sh. Commands executed: KnowBe4 API calls for user training data and enrollments

validators.name: Incident Response Training Validator
validators.statement: Confirms the completion rate of Role specific Incident Response and Disaster Recovery training.

okta.name: Okta
okta.description: Okta identity and access management evidence collection scripts
okta.access_method_description: The evidence fetcher scripts use the Okta REST API which requires OKTA_API_TOKEN and OKTA_ORG_URL environment variables. Authentication uses API Token authorization header.

okta_phishing_resistant_mfa.name: Okta Phishing-Resistant MFA (KSI-IAM-01)
okta_phishing_resistant_mfa.description: FedRAMP 20x KSI-IAM-01 evidence: phishing-resistant MFA configuration and adoption (WebAuthn/security keys).
okta_phishing_resistant_mfa.instructions: Script: okta_phishing_resistant_mfa.py. Uses Okta APIs to collect phishing-resistant MFA (FIDO2/WebAuthn) configuration and adoption evidence. Output: okta_phishing_resistant_mfa.json
solution_capabilities: Administrative Session: Okta IDaaS to Identity Center
Privileged Access: Amazon Identity and Access Management (IAM)
Service Account Permissions: Amazon Identity and Access Management (IAM)

validators.name: Okta Phishing Resistant MFA
validators.statement: This validator provides a portable baseline signal that phishing‑resistant MFA capability exists in Okta and that there is at least some real adoption, without requiring a FedRAMP‑specific security‑key‑only enforcement posture or 100% rollout. It reads the evidence summary to confirm that at least one phishing‑resistant authenticator type is configured (phishing_resistant_authenticator_types_count ≥ 1) and that at least one user is enrolled or using phishing‑resistant MFA (users_with_phishing_resistant_mfa > 0). Because it does not require full user coverage, specific key models, or additional FedRAMP‑specific constraints, it remains applicable across different organizational maturity levels and implementation choices.

validators.name: All Okta Users Have Security Key for Phishing Resistant MFA
validators.statement: This validator is intended to demonstrate a strict, FedRAMP‑aligned phishing‑resistant MFA posture by showing that phishing‑resistant authentication is configured, that the configured phishing‑resistant method includes security keys, and that phishing‑resistant MFA is applied across the full user population. It evaluates the evidence to confirm at least one phishing‑resistant authenticator type is configured (phishing_resistant_authenticator_types_count ≥ 1), that the organization’s configured phishing‑resistant methods explicitly include security_key (phishing_resistant_types contains security_key), and that enforcement is complete by requiring the number of users with phishing‑resistant MFA to equal the total number of users evaluated (users_with_phishing_resistant_mfa = total_users). It also corroborates organization‑wide coverage using the script’s computed metric (phishing_resistant_mfa_percentage = 100), while the count equality check prevents pass outcomes caused by rounding or formatting. The validator passes only when security‑key‑based phishing‑resistant MFA is both present and fully adopted, supporting an audit narrative that strong possession‑factor MFA is enforced consistently for all users.

okta_passwordless_authentication.name: Okta Passwordless Authentication (KSI-IAM-02)
okta_passwordless_authentication.description: FedRAMP 20x KSI-IAM-02 evidence: passwordless authentication enforcement and user enrollment (security keys / policy).
okta_passwordless_authentication.instructions: Script: okta_passwordless_authentication.py. Uses Okta APIs to collect passwordless authentication policy and enrollment evidence. Output: okta_passwordless_authentication.json
solution_capabilities: Administrative Session: Okta IDaaS to Identity Center
Privileged Access: Amazon Identity and Access Management (IAM)
Service Account Permissions: Amazon Identity and Access Management (IAM)

validators.name: Okta Passwordless Authentication-FedRAMP 20x Mod
validators.statement: This validator is intended to demonstrate a strict, FedRAMP‑aligned passwordless posture where passwordless authentication is not merely available, but is enforced specifically through security keys for application sign‑on and is fully adopted across the evaluated user population. It evaluates the evidence to confirm that the organization’s sign‑on policy explicitly requires security keys (security_key_enforced_for_application_sign_on = true), that a real population was assessed (total_active_users_checked > 0), and that every evaluated user is enrolled with a security key by requiring the enrolled count to equal the checked population (users_with_security_key_enrolled = total_active_users_checked). It also uses the script’s computed percentage (security_key_enrollment_percentage = 100) as an additional corroborating signal, while the count equality check prevents pass outcomes caused by rounding or formatting. The validator passes only when enforcement and full coverage are both demonstrated, supporting an audit narrative that passwordless access is implemented organization‑wide using a phishing‑resistant possession factor consistent with strict FedRAMP expectations.

validators.name: Okta Passwordless Authentication
validators.statement: This validator provides a portable baseline signal that the organization has at least one passwordless‑capable authenticator configured in Okta, without requiring FedRAMP’s security‑key‑only enforcement posture or organization‑wide adoption. It reads the evidence summary and extracts the number of configured passwordless authenticators (passwordless_authenticators_count), then passes if that value is at least one (passwordless_authenticators_count ≥ 1).

okta_non_user_accounts_authentication.name: Okta Non-User Accounts (KSI-IAM-03)
okta_non_user_accounts_authentication.description: FedRAMP 20x KSI-IAM-03 evidence: service/non-user accounts and automation authentication (API tokens, OAuth apps).
okta_non_user_accounts_authentication.instructions: Script: okta_non_user_accounts_authentication.py. Uses Okta APIs to identify non-user/service accounts using definitive indicators and automation credentials. Output: okta_non_user_accounts_authentication.json
solution_capabilities: Administrative Session: Okta IDaaS to Identity Center
Privileged Access: Amazon Identity and Access Management (IAM)
Service Account Permissions: Amazon Identity and Access Management (IAM)

validators.name: Okta Non-User Accounts Authentication-FedRAMP 20x Mod
validators.statement: This validator is intended to demonstrate a strict, FedRAMP‑aligned posture for non‑user (service/automation) identities by showing that service accounts exist, that automation credentials exist, that those credentials can be attributed to specific owning identities, and that at least one service account was identified through a strong and auditable linkage to real automation artifacts rather than naming heuristics. It evaluates the evidence to confirm that service accounts were detected (service_accounts_count), that API tokens used for non‑interactive automation are present (api_tokens_count), and that token owners were enumerated (api_token_owners_count) so automation credentials can be tied back to accountable identities. For strict assurance, it also requires evidence that at least one service account was identified specifically via the api_token_owner detection method (service_accounts_by_detection_method.api_token_owner), which strengthens the audit story by directly linking the non‑user identity population to concrete automation credentials discovered in Okta.

okta_just_in_time_authorization.name: Okta Just-in-Time Authorization (KSI-IAM-04)
okta_just_in_time_authorization.description: FedRAMP 20x KSI-IAM-04 evidence: conditional access, group-based assignments, and recent access activity (JIT authorization).
okta_just_in_time_authorization.instructions: Script: okta_just_in_time_authorization.py. Uses Okta APIs to collect JIT authorization evidence (policies, rules, group-based access, activity). Output: okta_just_in_time_authorization.json
solution_capabilities: Administrative Session: Okta IDaaS to Identity Center
Privileged Access: Amazon Identity and Access Management (IAM)
Service Account Permissions: Amazon Identity and Access Management (IAM)

validators.name: Okta Just-In-Time Authorization
validators.statement: This validator provides a portable baseline signal that the organization is collecting the key measurable indicators associated with “JIT‑ish” access patterns in Okta, without imposing maturity‑dependent thresholds. It checks that the evidence includes numeric fields for group‑based access coverage, the presence of conditional rules, the presence of contextual conditions within policies, and recent access events; in other words, it confirms the dataset contains the core telemetry needed to evaluate context‑aware authorization and group‑mediated access.

okta_least_privilege.name: Okta Least Privilege (KSI-IAM-05)
okta_least_privilege.description: FedRAMP 20x KSI-IAM-05 evidence: privileged admin population and least-privilege posture (admins vs regular users).
okta_least_privilege.instructions: Script: okta_least_privilege.py. Uses Okta APIs to identify admins and summarize privileged access. Output: okta_least_privilege.json
solution_capabilities: Administrative Session: Okta IDaaS to Identity Center
Privileged Access: Amazon Identity and Access Management (IAM)
Service Account Permissions: Amazon Identity and Access Management (IAM)

validators.name: Okta Least Privilege-FedRAMP 20x Mod
validators.statement: This validator is intended to demonstrate a strict, FedRAMP‑aligned least‑privilege posture for privileged administration in Okta by showing that privileged access is clearly defined, operationally viable, and free of unexpected admin role types. It evaluates the evidence to confirm the script assessed a real population (total_active_users > 0), that at least one Super Admin exists for secure operations and break‑glass administration (super_admin_count ≥ 1), and that at least one restricted read‑only admin identity exists to support least‑privilege operational patterns such as auditing or automation (read_only_admin_count ≥ 1). It further enforces that there are no “other” or uncategorized admin types (other_admin_count = 0), which supports an audit narrative that privileged roles are intentionally scoped and categorized rather than accruing ad‑hoc administrative access. Finally, it includes a sanity consistency check that the total number of admin users is not greater than the total evaluated users (admin_users_count ≤ total_active_users), ensuring the reported counts are coherent.

okta_suspicious_activity_management.name: Okta Suspicious Activity (KSI-IAM-06)
okta_suspicious_activity_management.description: FedRAMP 20x KSI-IAM-06 evidence: suspicious activity monitoring, security events, and behavior detection.
okta_suspicious_activity_management.instructions: Script: okta_suspicious_activity_management.py. Uses Okta APIs to collect security event monitoring and behavior detection evidence. Output: okta_suspicious_activity_management.json

validators.name: Okta Suspicious Activity Management-FedRAMP 20x Mod
validators.statement: This validator is intended to demonstrate a strict, FedRAMP‑aligned suspicious activity management posture by showing that the organization has multiple automated detection mechanisms enabled, that advanced anomaly/behavior detection is explicitly part of the monitoring program, and that monitoring is operational and producing auditable security telemetry. It evaluates the evidence to confirm that at least two detection mechanisms are configured (mechanisms_configured ≥ 2), that the configured mechanisms explicitly include “Behavior Detection” (showing anomaly detection is in scope, not only basic logging), and that behavior detection is actively configured through at least one active rule (active_rules ≥ 1). It also requires evidence that the monitoring pipeline is operational by checking for recent security event volume in the window (security_events_last_30_days > 0), and it validates that core suspicious-activity signals such as failed authentication attempts are being tracked as a numeric metric (failed_auth_attempts ≥ 0, optionally stricter if desired). The validator passes only when these conditions are met, supporting an audit narrative that suspicious activity detection is layered, configured, and actively producing monitoring data suitable for FedRAMP-level evidence.

okta_automated_account_management.name: Okta Automated Account Management (KSI-IAM-07)
okta_automated_account_management.description: FedRAMP 20x KSI-IAM-07 evidence: automated account lifecycle management and account health monitoring.
okta_automated_account_management.instructions: Script: okta_automated_account_management.py. Uses Okta APIs to collect account lifecycle monitoring and health evidence. Output: okta_automated_account_management.json

validators.name: Okta Automated Account Management-FedRAMP 20x Mod
validators.statement: This validator is intended to demonstrate strong, FedRAMP‑aligned automated account management hygiene by showing that the organization is actively managing its account population, maintaining high account “health,” and preventing long‑inactive accounts from persisting. It evaluates the evidence to confirm that a real user population was assessed (total_users > 0), that the environment is in a strict operational posture where all evaluated users are active (active_accounts = total_users), that overall account health is high (account_health_percentage ≥ 80, where “health” is defined by the script as recent login activity), and that there are no users who have been inactive for 90+ days (inactive_90_plus_days = 0). The validator passes only when these conditions are met, supporting an audit narrative that accounts are continuously governed, monitored, and kept within a clean lifecycle boundary consistent with strong compliance expectations.

gitlab.name: GitLab
gitlab.access_method_description: The evidence fetcher scripts use the GitLab REST API which requires project access toekns: GITLAB_PROJECT_*_API_ACCESS_TOKEN and GITLAB_PROJECT_*_URL environment variables for multi-project support. Authentication uses Bearer token with Project Access Tokens scoped to individual projects. Read permissions are required.

gitlab_ci_cd_pipeline_config.name: GitLab CI/CD Pipeline Configuration
gitlab_ci_cd_pipeline_config.description: Evidence for automated testing, security scanning, and deployment controls in .gitlab-ci.yml
gitlab_ci_cd_pipeline_config.instructions: Script: gitlab_ci_cd_pipeline_config.sh. Commands executed: GitLab API calls to retrieve project .gitlab-ci.yml and pipeline configuration

validators.name: CI/CD Pipeline Configuration Exists
validators.statement: Confirms that a GitLab CI/CD pipeline configuration is defined for the repository.

validators.name: Automated Functional Testing Executed
validators.statement: Confirms that automated tests are executed as part of the CI/CD pipeline.

validators.name: Grype CVE Suppression List Contains Only Approved Entries (Total Count)
validators.statement: Confirms the total number of suppressed vulnerability IDs in the Grype ignore config matches exactly the approved list. Any addition of an unapproved CVE or GHSA will increase the count and fail this check. Works in tandem with the approved-entries-only check.

validators.name: Pipeline Restricted to MRs and Protected Branches
validators.statement: Confirms the CI/CD pipeline is gated to only run on merge request events or protected branches preventing arbitrary pipeline execution outside of the controlled change process.

validators.name: Container Images Sourced from Private ECR Registry
validators.statement: Confirms that container images used in the pipeline are sourced from the organization's private AWS ECR registry rather than public registries ensuring image provenance and access control.

validators.name: Container Image Built with Kaniko (No Privileged Docker Daemon)
validators.statement: Confirms container images are built using Kaniko which eliminates the need for a privileged Docker socket reducing build-time attack surface and container escape risk.

validators.name: Container Vulnerability Scanning Enforces Medium+ Severity Threshold
validators.statement: Confirms container images are scanned with Grype and the pipeline is configured to fail on medium or higher severity vulnerabilities. Captures the severity level in group 1 and verifies it is set to 'medium' (not weakened to high or critical).

validators.name: Grype CVE Suppression List Contains Only Approved Entries (Approved IDs Only)
validators.statement: Confirms every suppressed vulnerability ID in the Grype ignore config is on the approved false-positive list. If an unapproved CVE or GHSA is substituted in, the approved-match count will drop below 108 and this check fails. Works in tandem with the total count check.

validators.name: Controlled Application Deployment via CI/CD Pipeline
validators.statement: Confirms that application deployments are performed exclusively through the CI/CD pipeline using kubectl image updates ensuring all production changes flow through the automated controlled deployment process.

validators.name: Deployment Health Verified via Rollout Status Check
validators.statement: Confirms the pipeline actively monitors Kubernetes rollout status after each deployment and fails if pods do not reach a healthy state providing automated deployment verification.

validators.name: Automated Functional Tests Executed in Pipeline
validators.statement: Confirms automated tests run in the CI/CD pipeline before deployment. Applies to Node.js (npm run test:build:unit) and .NET (dotnet test) projects. Admission controller has no automated test job - a known gap.

validators.name: Pipeline Base Images Pinned to Immutable SHA256 Digest
validators.statement: Confirms that base images used within the CI/CD pipeline are pinned to a specific immutable SHA256 digest rather than a mutable tag. Admission controller currently uses mutable tags - a known gap.

gitlab_project_summary.name: GitLab Project Summary
gitlab_project_summary.description: Evidence for repository configuration inventory including Terraform, Docker, and YAML configs
gitlab_project_summary.instructions: Script: gitlab_project_summary.sh. Commands executed: GitLab API to list repository tree and file metadata

gitlab_merge_request_summary.name: GitLab Merge Request Summary
gitlab_merge_request_summary.description: Evidence for change management: approvals, reviews, discussions, and compliance metrics
gitlab_merge_request_summary.instructions: Script: gitlab_merge_request_summary.sh. Commands executed: GitLab API to list merge requests, approvals, and discussions with pagination

validators.name: Change Management Active
validators.statement: Validates that change management is active, approval process is enabled, and that it meets minimum review.

rippling.name: Rippling
rippling.access_method_description: The evidence fetcher scripts use the Rippling REST API which requires RIPPLING_API_TOKEN environment variable. Authentication uses Bearer token authorization.

rippling_current_employees.name: Rippling Current Employees
rippling_current_employees.description: Evidence for list of current active employees from Rippling
rippling_current_employees.instructions: Script: rippling_current_employees.py. Commands executed: curl GET /platform/api/employees with limit/offset pagination

rippling_all_employees.name: Rippling All Employees
rippling_all_employees.description: Evidence for list of all employees from Rippling including terminated
rippling_all_employees.instructions: Script: rippling_all_employees.py. Commands executed: curl GET /platform/api/employees/include_terminated with limit/offset pagination

checkov.name: Checkov Security Scanning
checkov.description: Infrastructure as Code security compliance scanning with Checkov
checkov.access_method: Checkov CLI with optional GitLab API
checkov.access_method_description: The evidence fetcher scripts use Checkov CLI tool which must be installed via 'pip install checkov'. For GitLab repository scanning, GitLab API access tokens are required (GITLAB_PROJECT_*_API_ACCESS_TOKEN). For local or URL-based scanning, no additional authentication is needed.

checkov_terraform.name: Checkov Terraform Security
checkov_terraform.description: Evidence for Terraform infrastructure security compliance using Checkov
checkov_terraform.instructions: Script: checkov_terraform.sh. Commands executed: checkov --framework terraform with flexible configuration options. Supports multiple source types (GitLab, GitHub, local, URL) and customizable check filtering.

validators.name: Infrastructure-as-Code Compliance Scan (Terraform)
validators.statement: Confirms the evidence is a Checkov scan targeting Terraform infrastructure-as-code.

validators.name: Compliance Threshold Met (≥ 84%) (Terraform)
validators.statement: Passes only if the overall Terraform compliance score is 84% or higher.

checkov_kubernetes.name: Checkov Kubernetes Security
checkov_kubernetes.description: Evidence for Kubernetes manifest security compliance using Checkov
checkov_kubernetes.instructions: Script: checkov_kubernetes.sh. Commands executed: checkov --framework kubernetes with flexible configuration options. Supports multiple source types (GitLab, GitHub, local, URL) and customizable check filtering.

validators.name: Kubernetes Configuration Compliance Scan
validators.statement: Validates that a Checkov scan was performed against Kubernetes manifests to assess configuration security compliance.

validators.name: Compliance Threshold Met (≥ 85%) (Kubernetes)
validators.statement: Passes only if the Kubernetes configuration compliance score meets or exceeds 85%.

ssllabs.name: Qualys SSL Labs
ssllabs.description: TLS/SSL configuration and certificate scanning via the Qualys SSL Labs API v4
ssllabs.access_method: Qualys SSL Labs API v4 with registered email
ssllabs.access_method_description: Scans are performed via the public Qualys SSL Labs API v4. A one-time email registration is required at https://api.ssllabs.com/api/v4/register. No credentials beyond the registered email are needed.

ssllabs_tls_scan.name: SSL Labs TLS/SSL Scan
ssllabs_tls_scan.description: Scans configured hosts via the Qualys SSL Labs API v4 and collects TLS grade, certificate validity, protocol support, and vulnerability evidence
ssllabs_tls_scan.instructions: Script: ssllabs_tls_scan.py. Calls the Qualys SSL Labs API v4 for each host in SSLLABS_HOSTS, polls until complete, and saves per-host JSON and a combined summary. Requires SSLLABS_EMAIL (registered with Qualys) and SSLLABS_HOSTS (comma-separated hostnames).
